Operation 'apikeys.projects.locations.keys.create' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary is empty, making it impossible for AI agents to understand the operation's purpose. Description mentions the global location constraint but lacks critical details about inputs (V2Key schema structure), outputs (newly created key object), and side effects (key generation). Enhanced version clarifies that this creates a key for a project, explains the global location requirement, describes the request/response schemas in domain context, and explains the key generation side effect. This enables AI agents to determine when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.current_description: Creates a new API key. NOTE: Key is a global resource; hence the only supported value for location is `global`.
data.summary_suggestion: Create a new API key for a project
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new API key associated with the specified project. API keys are global resources, so the location must be set to 'global'. The request body should contain the V2Key object with the desired key configuration, including any restrictions (API targets, browser referrers, Android/iOS apps, or server IP addresses). Returns the newly created V2Key object with a generated key string. This operation generates a unique key identifier and secret that can be used for API authentication.
data.reasoning: Original summary is empty, making it impossible for AI agents to understand the operation's purpose. Description mentions the global location constraint but lacks critical details about inputs (V2Key schema structure), outputs (newly created key object), and side effects (key generation). Enhanced version clarifies that this creates a key for a project, explains the global location requirement, describes the request/response schemas in domain context, and explains the key generation side effect. This enables AI agents to determine when to use this operation and what to expect.

message: Operation 'apikeys.keys.lookupKey' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context. Missing summary entirely. Description is clear but could better explain the input (key string) and output (lookup response). Added explicit mention of what is returned to improve AI agent understanding of when to use this operation.

data.current_description: Find the parent project and resource name of the API key that matches the key string in the request. If the API key has been purged, resource name will not be set. The service account must have the `apikeys.keys.lookup` permission on the parent project.
data.summary_suggestion: Find parent project and resource name for API key
data.description_suggestion: Finds the parent project and resource name of the API key matching the provided key string. Returns the lookup result with parent project and resource name. If the API key has been purged, the resource name will not be set. Requires the `apikeys.keys.lookup` permission on the parent project.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description is clear but could better explain the input (key string) and output (lookup response). Added explicit mention of what is returned to improve AI agent understanding of when to use this operation.

message: Operation 'apikeys.projects.locations.keys.delete' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_side_effects. Missing summary. Description lacks clarity about the soft-delete behavior and recovery mechanism. Enhanced to explain the two-phase deletion process and reference the undelete operation for better AI agent workflow understanding.

data.current_description: Deletes an API key. Deleted key can be retrieved within 30 days of deletion. Afterward, key will be purged from the project. NOTE: Key is a global resource; hence the only supported value for location is `global`.
data.summary_suggestion: Delete an API key with 30-day recovery window
data.description_suggestion: Deletes an API key from the project. The deleted key can be recovered using the undelete operation within 30 days. After 30 days, the key is permanently purged and cannot be recovered. This operation marks the key as deleted but does not immediately remove it. Note: API keys are global resources; the only supported location value is `global`.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description lacks clarity about the soft-delete behavior and recovery mechanism. Enhanced to explain the two-phase deletion process and reference the undelete operation for better AI agent workflow understanding.

data.current_description: Patches the modifiable fields of an API key. The key string of the API key isn't included in the response. NOTE: Key is a global resource; hence the only supported value for location is `global`.
data.summary_suggestion: Update modifiable fields of an API key
data.description_suggestion: Updates the modifiable fields of an API key using a PATCH operation. Accepts a V2Key object with the fields to be updated; only provided fields will be modified. The key string is not included in the response. Returns the updated V2Key object. Note: API keys are global resources; the only supported location value is `global`.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description lacks clarity about partial update semantics, input schema, and what is returned. Enhanced to explain PATCH behavior, input/output schemas, and the exclusion of key string for better AI agent understanding of update operations.

data.current_description: Undeletes an API key which was deleted within 30 days. NOTE: Key is a global resource; hence the only supported value for location is `global`.
data.summary_suggestion: Recover a deleted API key within 30 days
data.description_suggestion: Recovers an API key that was previously deleted within the last 30 days. Accepts a V2UndeleteKeyRequest and restores the key to an active state. Returns the restored V2Key object. Keys deleted more than 30 days ago cannot be recovered. Note: API keys are global resources; the only supported location value is `global`.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description lacks clarity about the recovery window constraint, input/output schemas, and the restoration effect. Enhanced to explain the time-bound recovery capability and what is returned for better AI agent understanding of recovery workflows.

data.current_description: Lists the API keys owned by a project. The key string of the API key isn't included in the response. NOTE: Key is a global resource; hence the only supported value for location is `global`.
data.summary_suggestion: List all API keys for a project
data.description_suggestion: Lists all API keys owned by a specified project. Returns a V2ListKeysResponse containing a collection of V2Key objects. The key string is not included in the response for security reasons. Supports pagination through standard list parameters. Note: API keys are global resources; the only supported location value is `global`.
